title: Counting and Natural Numbers
statement: Discrete quantities can be represented consistently by assigning ordered count markers to distinguishable items.
review_notes: Clarified counting as one-to-one assignment between distinguishable items and ordered markers.
Clarified dependency on UTE-FV-0002 as equality/correspondence of quantity.
Added boundary distinction from measurement, ordering, ranking, labelling, and approximation.
Future decomposition may separate counting, natural number sequence, successor, zero, cardinality, and ordinal position.
